steps.title: Spot the noun phrase structure
steps.content: On the left of the blank: 'geologist,' a noun that is part of the noun phrase 'American geologist.' On the right: 'Marie Tharp,' a proper noun followed by a comma and an appositive phrase.

steps.title: Apply the punctuation rule
steps.content: No punctuation should separate noun modifiers from the proper names they describe, so choice B correctly leaves 'geologist' and 'Marie Tharp' together, with the comma appearing after the complete unit to introduce the appositive. Choices A, C, and D all insert punctuation that breaks this rule. That matches choice B — B is correct.
